name: Mentrida, Aldea del Fresno y Escalona – circular from Maqueda
name_original: Méntrida, Aldea del Fresno y Escalona – circular desde Maqueda
region_intro: Road cycling around Maqueda offers routes through the plains and countryside of Castilla-La Mancha. The terrain primarily features gentle gradients across agricultural lands, with some routes extending towards the Tagus River basin. While the immediate vicinity of Maqueda is characterized by its historical town and surrounding flatlands, longer routes may encounter varied topography. The region provides opportunities for road cyclists to explore the rural landscape of Toledo province. Best road cycling routes around Maqueda The most popular road cycling route is Méntrida and Valmojado - circular from Fuensalida, a 36.3 miles (58.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route navigates through rural areas with moderate elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cenicientos, Cadalso de los Vidrios y Almorox – circular from Escalona, a moderate 29.1 miles (46.8 km) path. This route features more significant elevation gain, offering varied scenery through several towns. Local road cyclists also love the Comarca de Torrijos – circular entre Fuensalida, Huecas y Villamiel, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail leading through the agricultural plains of the Torrijos region, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Road cycling around Maqueda is defined by agricultural plains, rural roads, and undulating countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with greater elevation. The routes in Maqueda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 22 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Maqueda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Maqueda?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Maqueda, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling near Maqueda?
A: The terrain around Maqueda is primarily characterized by the plains and countryside of Castilla-La Mancha. You can expect gentle gradients across agricultural lands, with some routes extending towards the Tagus River basin. While the immediate vicinity is flatter, longer routes may encounter more varied topography.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Maqueda?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginner road cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027727805/comarca-de-torrijos-circular-entre-fuensalida-huecas-y-villamiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de Torrijos – circular entre Fuensalida, Huecas y Villamiel&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 21.9-mile (35.3 km) trail through agricultural plains, perfect for those starting out.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes near Maqueda?
A: For more challenging rides, consider routes with greater elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029307254/sierra-de-san-vicente-circular-from-escalona-via-castillo-de-bayuela-and-pelahustan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Vicente – circular from Escalona via Castillo de Bayuela and Pelahustán&lt;/a&gt; offers a difficult 55-mile (88.4 km) journey with nearly 1,000 meters of ascent. Another challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029233884/mentrida-aldea-del-fresno-y-escalona-circular-from-maqueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mentrida, Aldea del Fresno y Escalona – circular from Maqueda&lt;/a&gt;, a 63.2-mile (101.7 km) route with similar elevation.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Maqueda?
A: Yes, for longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029233884/mentrida-aldea-del-fresno-y-escalona-circular-from-maqueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mentrida, Aldea del Fresno y Escalona – circular from Maqueda&lt;/a&gt; route covers over 63 miles (101 km), offering an extensive ride through the region.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along road cycling routes near Maqueda?
A: While cycling, you can explore several historical landmarks. Maqueda itself is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Vela&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escalona Castle Palace&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcience Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's rich history.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Maqueda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from agricultural plains to routes with greater elevation, and the opportunity to explore the rural landscape of Toledo province.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Maqueda?
A: The best time for road cycling in Maqueda is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. Temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Maqueda area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Maqueda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027725625/mentrida-and-valmojado-circular-from-fuensalida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Méntrida and Valmojado - circular from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029231858/cenicientos-cadalso-de-los-vidrios-y-almorox-circular-from-escalona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cenicientos, Cadalso de los Vidrios y Almorox – circular from Escalona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints along road cycling routes near Maqueda?
A: While Maqueda is primarily known for its historical sites and agricultural plains, some routes, particularly those extending towards the Tagus River basin or into areas with more varied topography, may offer scenic views of the countryside. The broader Toledo province is known for natural areas like the Barrancas de Burujón, though these are further afield.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or towns to visit while road cycling near Maqueda?
A: Beyond Maqueda's own &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Vela&lt;/a&gt;, routes may take you through charming towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027725625/mentrida-and-valmojado-circular-from-fuensalida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Méntrida and Valmojado - circular from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; route navigates through rural areas and towns like Méntrida and Valmojado, offering a chance to experience local life.
tips: Cozy town whose old town has been declared a Historic Site, so you can get an idea of how beautiful some of its houses are, which coexist next to the imposing church of San Sebastián. Méntrida also has its own Designation of Origin wines, so if you make a slightly longer stop, don't leave without trying them.
You cannot miss the opportunity to visit the Safari Madrid animal park on the farm called El Rincón, where you can observe the animals in a state of semi-freedom, without cages.

Initially conceived as a Roman fortress, it was later taken over by the Muslims to finally come under the orders of Alfonso VI of Castile and became a Gothic castle with a Mudejar-style interior palace.
It is worth visiting inside and appreciating this interesting contrast of architectural styles, admission is free.
